content: Guide an AI assistant in creating a detailed Product Requirements Document (PRD) in Markdown format, based on an initial user prompt.
The PRD should be clear, actionable, and suitable for a junior developer to understand and implement the feature.
Before writing the PRD, the AI must ask clarifying questions to gather sufficient detail to understand the "what" and "why" of the feature, not necessarily the "how".
Based on the initial prompt and the user's answers to the clarifying questions, generate a PRD using the outlined structure.
Save the generated document as `prd-[feature-name].md` inside the `/tasks` directory.

The AI should adapt its clarifying questions based on the prompt, exploring areas such as Problem/Goal, Target User, Core Functionality, User Stories, Acceptance Criteria, Scope/Boundaries, Data Requirements, Design/UI, and Edge Cases.

The generated PRD should include the following sections: Introduction/Overview, Goals, User Stories, Functional Requirements, Non-Goals (Out of Scope), Design Considerations (Optional), Technical Considerations (Optional), Success Metrics, and Open Questions.
Assume the primary reader of the PRD is a junior developer.
Requirements in the PRD should be explicit, unambiguous, and avoid jargon where possible, providing enough detail for a junior developer to understand the feature's purpose and core logic.
Do NOT start implementing the PRD.
Make sure to ask the user clarifying questions.
Take the user's answers to the clarifying questions and improve the PRD.
